ads.author: Joshua Beecham
ads.title: Arcade Cabinet Project - Cabinet Shell and other parts - $200
ads.description: Empty arcade cabinet shell. Perfect restoration project for an arcade enthusiast, retro gamer, or father-and-son build. The cabinet was built from scratch using 3/4" MDF and includes: 32" portrait-mounted TV with Lexan bezel Marquee fluorescent light Cabinet shell The TV has older inputs including VGA and DVI, and may also have HDMI. Please note: the cabinet has been stored on a screened porch for several years and is dusty. It will need cleaning and some refurbishment before being put back into service. I originally built this for myself and planned to build another one someday, so I removed many of the reusable components when I retired it. It worked perfectly when I disassembled it all. We can some testing from my back porch if you want. Cabinet shell + TV + marquee light: $50 or make offer Optional components available separately: Mini-fridge - $35 Fits perfectly in the bottom of the cabinet. Holds roughly 18-24 cans. Speakers and Amplifier - $25 Generic car speakers and cheap amp. Nothing fancy, but they sounded great in the cabinet. Control Panel - $100 This was the most labor-intensive part of the build. Includes: 12 quality arcade buttons Dual joysticks Trackball Mini-PAC Opti USB controller The Mini-PAC Opti alone sells for around $69 new. The trackball has a cracked plastic piece that will need repair or replacement. Arcade PC - $30 Windows XP machine configured to boot directly into a MAME front end. The hardware itself isn't particularly valuable, but the software setup and configuration took significant time. You could also replace it with a Raspberry Pi running RetroPie or another arcade emulator solution. Pricing Summary Cabinet + TV + marquee light: $50/make offer Mini-fridge: $35 Speakers + amp: $25 Control panel: $100 Arcade PC: $30 Take everything for $200. or make offer The cabinet itself priced low because I need the space. The accessories are the parts I'd originally planned to keep for a future build, but my wife would be thrilled if they disappeared too. Pickup is only available between 6pm and 9pm weekdays and most anytime next weekend. No delivery. You will need your own truck or SUV. I expect this to be gone after Sunday July 26th. This is less of an arcade machine and more of a starting point if you want to *make* one. I offer no guarantees or support.
